message.abstract:
<jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background (traditional theory)</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    there are general and principle models and calculation formulas for epidemics, mainly the basic reproduction number R
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    of a certain epidemic disease such as COVID-19, the newly confirmed number C
                    <jats:sub>n</jats:sub>
                    =R
                    <jats:sub>n-1</jats:sub>
                    C
                    <jats:sub>n-1</jats:sub>
                    , The threshold of herd immunity is (R
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    -1)/R
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    .
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Innovation theory</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Based on the fact that there are groups of different tends (that is, different R
                    <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                    or R
                    <jats:sub>k</jats:sub>
                    ), a grouping model of epidemiology is proposed, and a complete and detailed calculation formula is given. The basic relationship is that the overall infection numbers of is equal to the sum of the infection number of groups, namely: R
                    <jats:sub>t</jats:sub>
                    =∑(f
                    <jats:sub>k</jats:sub>
                    R
                    <jats:sub>k</jats:sub>
                    ), where f
                    <jats:sub>k</jats:sub>
                    is the population proportion of each group, and R
                    <jats:sub>k</jats:sub>
                    is the basic reproduction number of each groups. Its important application is the grouping strategy in which prevention and control measures are inclined to high-risk groups. The basic relationship is that the basic reproduction number of each group is equal to the product of the control coefficient of its measures and the original basic reproduction number, namely: R
                    <jats:sub>k</jats:sub>
                    =ζ
                    <jats:sub>k</jats:sub>
                    R
                    <jats:sub>0k</jats:sub>
                    . Compared with the traditional strategy of equal treatment, the grouping strategy has the characteristics of high efficiency, low consumption, and low threshold of herd immunity.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Grouping theory of epidemiology
